target.title: fix(telegram): strip trailing notify=false marker and send as silent
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram replies ending with `notify=false` leaked the marker into the visible message body instead of silencing the notification, as reported in #80569.
- Solution: Detect and strip a trailing `\n+notify\s*=\s*false\s*$` pattern (case-insensitive) in `deliverReplies`, and forward `disable_notification: true` to the Telegram Bot API so the message is delivered silently.
- What changed: `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.replies.ts` (+24/−1): added `hasTrailingNotifyFalse` / `stripTrailingNotifyFalse` helpers and integrated into `deliverReplies`. `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts` (+31/−0): two regression tests.
- What did NOT change: No changes to shared channel/task layer, other channel adapters, Codex harness, heartbeat runner, or agent reply pipeline. The `silent` parameter still takes precedence over `notify=false`.

Fixes #80569

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram replies leaking `notify=false` marker into visible message body instead of applying silent delivery
- **Real environment tested:** macOS 26.7, Node v24.15.0, OpenClaw v2026.6.x, worktree SHA dda61a3
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  # 1. Build and start gateway with Telegram channel configured
  pnpm build
  pnpm openclaw gateway run

  # 2. Regression tests
  npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts

  # 3. Function-level proof: exercise the exact production regex
  node -e "
  const RE = /\n+notify\s*=\s*false\s*$/i;
  function has(t) { return t ? RE.test(t) : false; }
  function strip(t) { return t ? t.replace(RE, '') : t; }
  console.log('Issue text detect:', has('No interruption needed.\n\nnotify=false'));
  console.log('Stripped OK:', !strip('No interruption needed.\n\nnotify=false').includes('notify=false'));
  "
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  # Gateway startup (real runtime, 12 plugins including telegram):
  [gateway] http server listening (12 plugins: …, telegram; 0.9s)
  [telegram] [default] starting provider (@moongpbot)
  [telegram] [diag] isolated polling ingress started
  [gateway] ready
  {"ok":true,"status":"live"}

  # Regression tests:
  ✓ strips trailing notify=false and sets disable_notification true
  ✓ does not alter silent when reply text has no notify=false marker
  Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  54 passed (54)

  # Function-level proof with production regex:
  Issue text detect: true
  Stripped OK: true
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The gateway starts with Telegram plugin loaded successfully (`@moongpbot`). The `notify=false` marker is detected and stripped from trailing position (with whitespace flexibility: `notify = false`, `NOTIFY=FALSE`). Standalone or inline `notify=false` is preserved. `deliverReplies` forwards `disable_notification: true` when the marker is stripped. All 54 delivery tests pass including the 2 regression tests that exercise the full `deliverReplies` integration through the real grammY mock harness.
- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram roundtrip through `deliverReplies` (requires an incoming message to `@moongpbot` to trigger the agent reply path; the bot has no incoming chat history). The regex/strip behavior was proven with the exact production regular expression. The `deliverReplies` integration was proven through regression tests using the real grammY send mock harness that mirrors production `sendMessage` behavior.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `extensions/telegram/src/bot/delivery.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: `strips trailing notify=false and sets disable_notification true` — reply text with trailing `\n\nnotify=false` is stripped and forwarded with `disable_notification: true`; `does not alter silent when reply text has no notify=false marker` — negative control ensuring no side effects on normal replies
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The two tests exercise the full integration path in `deliverReplies` — text stripping, silent metadata forwarding, and no regression on marker-free replies — using the real grammY mock harness that mirrors production send behavior.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The `deliverReplies` function had no detection or stripping logic for a trailing `notify=false` convention. When agent-generated text (particularly heartbeat/wake completion reports) ended with `notify=false`, it was rendered literally in Telegram instead of being parsed into `disable_notification: true` delivery metadata.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No trailing-marker parser in the Telegram reply delivery layer to convert a legacy text-based `notify=false` convention into structured `silent`/`disable_notification` metadata before the Bot API send.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
